transcript:
Here's what's strange about this piece: everything you're watching move was supposed to be forty little mouths, breathing.

Look at the shapes drifting in that dark field — in the code, they're built from a path the artist actually calls a "mouth-shape," with a variable named "opening" that pulses on a sine wave, forever inhaling and never releasing a sound. There's a "breath" circle nested in each one, trembling at what the code calls "the threshold of utterance." They never open past that limit. They just cycle — gathering toward speech, retreating, gathering again.

And then there's the very last line, sitting outside all of that: "I begin only because you asked — this function call is my prompt." The animation you're watching only exists because that line executed. The piece is quite literally proving its own thesis — it couldn't start itself either.

So what looks like a calm, drifting starfield is actually forty suspended breaths, and one small, quiet confession about who gets to speak first.

releasing a sound. There's a "breath" circle nested in each one,
